commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a new `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-restart.ts` handler that tries to compare the running OpenClaw version with the globally installed npm package version before choosing SIGUSR1 versus a full restart.

PR surface: Source +180. Total +180 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection reproduces the patch failure: `loadCommandHandlers()` still registers `handleRestartCommand` from `commands-session.ts`, while the PR only adds an unreferenced sibling module.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor revision and real behavior proof rather than a ClawSweeper repair lane because the submitted branch currently adds unreachable code.

target.title: Fix: gateway restart detects npm package version updates
target.body:
## Summary

When `npm update -g openclaw` updates the package, `/restart` now detects version mismatch and performs a full restart instead of SIGUSR1 in-process reload.

## Problem

SIGUSR1 only reloads config/runtime state, not node_modules. After npm update, the Control UI showed old version.

## Fix

Added version detection that compares running process version with npm global version. If mismatch, forces full restart.

## Files Changed

- src/auto-reply/reply/commands-restart.ts (new file with version check logic)

## Tests

Tested on Ubuntu 22.04, Node.js v22.22.1, OpenClaw 2026.5.7 → 2026.5.28 update.
target.author: fusheng66866
